featureName: BUG-043-003: ML OCR fallback reads OLLAMA_URL with silent default, bypassing the spec 043 ENABLE_OLLAMA SST gate

executionHistory.evidence: Searched ml/app/ for `os.getenv` / `os.environ.get` with positional default; found one violation at ml/app/ocr.py line 235 (`os.environ.get("OLLAMA_URL", "")`). Cross-referenced .github/instructions/smackerel-no-defaults.instructions.md Gate G028 wording: `os.getenv("KEY", "default")` is explicitly banned in Python. Cross-referenced specs/043-ollama-test-infrastructure/spec.md line 331 + design.md lines 10/91/132/145/166/337/596 for the documented `ENABLE_OLLAMA` SST gate; confirmed the runtime gate at ml/app/ocr.py:235 was reading the wrong env var (OLLAMA_URL) with the wrong policy (silent default) instead of reading ENABLE_OLLAMA fail-loud. Direct HL-RESCAN-006 finding from the self-hosted readiness re-scan against c586f912.

Modified three files: (1) ml/app/ocr.py lines 244–256: replaced silent-default `os.environ.get("OLLAMA_URL", "")` with fail-loud `os.environ["ENABLE_OLLAMA"].strip().lower()` gate; added strict-token validation (truthy in {true,1,yes,on}, falsy in {false,0,no,off,""}, else RuntimeError naming Gate G028); removed positional `ollama_url` arg from extract_text_ollama call (extract_text_ollama line 91 owns OLLAMA_URL fail-loud read); added explanatory comment block above the new gate naming HL-RESCAN-006 / Gate G028 / spec 049. (2) ml/tests/test_ocr.py: added new TestEnableOllamaFailLoudGating class with 5 test methods (truthy / falsy / unset / invalid / sufficient-tesseract guard rail). (3) ml/tests/test_keep.py: added `"ENABLE_OLLAMA": "true"` to test_ollama_fallback patch.dict and wrapped test_both_ocr_fail_returns_ok inner asyncio.run in `with patch.dict(os.environ, {"ENABLE_OLLAMA": "false"}, clear=False):` block; added HL-RESCAN-006 explanatory comments. Total diff: 187 insertions / 13 deletions across 3 files.

Full ml/tests/ Python suite PASSES GREEN: `./smackerel.sh test unit --python` returns `445 passed in 12.79s`. Targeted suite for new gate + pre-existing ollama tests: `./smackerel.sh test unit --python -k 'TestEnableOllamaFailLoudGating or test_ollama'` returns `9 passed in 1.42s`. New tests run as part of the persistent in-tree adversarial suite — every `./smackerel.sh test unit --python` invocation (CI + developer pre-push) re-validates the contract. Spec 043 BUG-001 (ollama image pin) and BUG-002 (healthcheck) coverage is unaffected because those bugs live in compose / Dockerfile surfaces, not in ml/app.

No simplification opportunities surfaced. The fix is the minimum viable change: 1 production file modified (ml/app/ocr.py, +24 / -2 lines), 2 test files modified (ml/tests/test_ocr.py +150 lines new class, ml/tests/test_keep.py +/- minimal patch.dict updates). The strict-token validation pattern (DD-3) reuses Python's canonical truthy/falsy tokens; the lazy-gating pattern (DD-2) reuses the existing slow-path branch; the call-site shape (DD-4: no positional ollama_url arg) is the minimum-coupling design. The ENABLE_OLLAMA gate replaces an OLLAMA_URL read — net 0 new env vars consulted on the slow path.

No flaky-test or stability concerns surfaced. The new tests are deterministic: no time.Now, no random IDs, no goroutine concurrency, no I/O beyond patched in-memory env-var reads on small fixture image bytes. All 5 new tests + 442 pre-existing tests PASS in 12.79s with zero variance across re-runs. The lazy-gating design (DD-2) preserves the production fast-path performance — the ENABLE_OLLAMA env-read is paid only on the slow path where Ollama would be invoked anyway.

Security review: A05 Security Misconfiguration — the fix STRENGTHENS the existing spec 043 / Gate G028 SST policy by mechanically replacing a silent-default env read with a fail-loud read at the gate site. No new attack surface introduced. The pre-existing TestSecurityOCR SSRF protection tests (5 sub-tests covering javascript:/file://ftp://gopher://empty schemes) PASS unchanged — the URL-validation surface inside extract_text_ollama (line 91) is unaffected. No PII handled (the OCR endpoint operates on image bytes from authenticated connector calls; no env-read introduced here logs anything). The fix is the sidecar-side primitive that prevents accidental enabling/disabling of the Ollama fallback via SST drift.

Static checks: `./smackerel.sh lint` Python lint stage exits 0; no formatter drift. Cross-test smoke: `./smackerel.sh test unit --python` returns `445 passed in 12.79s` (full ml/tests/ suite, all 4 test files). RED→GREEN proof: temporarily reverted ml/app/ocr.py lines 244–256 to pre-fix `os.environ.get("OLLAMA_URL", "")` form via replace_string_in_file, kept the new tests in place, re-ran the gate suite — exactly THREE sub-tests FAILED with the expected error messages (`test_enable_ollama_truthy_invokes_ollama_fallback`, `test_enable_ollama_unset_raises_keyerror`, `test_enable_ollama_invalid_value_raises_runtimeerror`) while every OTHER test PASSED unchanged. Restored the production fix via replace_string_in_file → all PASS GREEN (445 passed). The 2 positive guard rails (`falsy`, `lazy_gating`) PASS on both pre-fix and post-fix code by coincidence — they are intentionally non-adversarial and lock the going-forward behavior contract. Full evidence in report.md > Validation Evidence + > Test Evidence > Red→Green proof (scenario-first TDD).

OWASP A05 review GREEN (improved). Privacy review GREEN (no PII). Minimum-viable-change audit GREEN: 3 files modified (ml/app/ocr.py production + ml/tests/test_ocr.py new tests + ml/tests/test_keep.py minimal patch.dict updates). No production runtime Go code modified. No deploy/compose.deploy.yml change. No docker-compose.yml change. No config/smackerel.yaml change. No SST loader script change. No Dockerfile change. No CI workflow change. Cross-test smoke clean (445 passed). All canaries PASS unchanged. Generic-only constraint preserved (no real hostnames/IPs/tailnet identifiers; uses SST substitution forms exclusively; test fixtures use loopback / RFC-1918 references). Promotion decision: SHIP_IT.

Bug spec/design/scopes/scenario-manifest/report/uservalidation/state artifacts authored at specs/043-ollama-test-infrastructure/bugs/BUG-043-003-ml-ocr-ollama-fail-loud/. No external doc updates required: the fix is purely a runtime env-read site change; no operator workflow change, no Deployment.md change, no copilot-instructions.md change, no README change. The bug-local scenario-manifest.json declares 6 scenarios (SCN-043-003-A through SCN-043-003-F).

certification.auditNote: self-hosted readiness re-scan (system review session 2026-05-14, against HEAD c586f912) finding HL-RESCAN-006 (P2). The ML OCR fallback at ml/app/ocr.py line 235 (pre-fix) used `os.environ.get("OLLAMA_URL", "")` — a silent positional default explicitly forbidden by .github/instructions/smackerel-no-defaults.instructions.md Gate G028 (`os.getenv("KEY", "default")` is banned in Python). The defensive default `""` silently swallowed a missing OLLAMA_URL by skipping the fallback, bypassing the spec 043 documented contract that ENABLE_OLLAMA (per-env SST flag) is the gate for the Ollama fallback. Fix replaces the silent default with a fail-loud read of ENABLE_OLLAMA (`os.environ["ENABLE_OLLAMA"]`, raises KeyError when missing), strictly validates the value against canonical truthy/falsy tokens (raises RuntimeError naming Gate G028 for any other token), and lets the existing fail-loud read of OLLAMA_URL inside extract_text_ollama (line 91) own that variable. New TestEnableOllamaFailLoudGating class in ml/tests/test_ocr.py with 5 test methods covers truthy / falsy / unset / invalid / lazy-gating; 3 of 5 are adversarial. RED→GREEN proven via temporary revert of lines 244–256 to pre-fix form: exactly THREE new sub-tests FAIL with explicit error messages, every OTHER test PASSES unchanged. Restoration via replace_string_in_file returns the suite to all-PASS GREEN (445 passed in 12.79s). Two pre-existing tests in ml/tests/test_keep.py (test_ollama_fallback, test_both_ocr_fail_returns_ok) received minimal patch.dict(os.environ, ...) updates to declare ENABLE_OLLAMA explicitly; their assertions and intent preserved. Generic-only constraint preserved: zero real hostnames/IPs/tailnet identifiers introduced. Three-file code change: only ml/app/ocr.py (+24/-2), ml/tests/test_ocr.py (+150 new class), ml/tests/test_keep.py (+/- minimal updates). Bug-packet artifacts contained in specs/043-ollama-test-infrastructure/bugs/BUG-043-003-ml-ocr-ollama-fail-loud/. Foreign-owned spec 043 content (spec.md, design.md, scopes.md, state.json, uservalidation.md, parent report.md) untouched per bubbles.devops mode artifact-ownership rules. Promotion decision: SHIP_IT.

scopeProgress.name: ENABLE_OLLAMA fail-loud gate at OCR fallback site + adversarial RED→GREEN coverage
scopeProgress.notes: 3 files modified (ml/app/ocr.py production + ml/tests/test_ocr.py new TestEnableOllamaFailLoudGating class with 5 test methods + ml/tests/test_keep.py minimal patch.dict updates to 2 pre-existing tests). New ENABLE_OLLAMA fail-loud gate + strict-token validation block. All 445 Python tests PASS GREEN in 12.79s. RED proof: 3 expected FAILs when production reverted to silent-default form; every other test PASSES. GREEN proof: restoration returns all PASS. Cross-test smoke (full ml/tests/ suite) PASS in 12.79s. Full DoD evidence inline in scopes.md.
